div.p: At its meeting on May 5, 1949, the National Security Council considered a draft report on proposed measures to achieve an agreed minimum list of goods not to be included in exports from Western European countries to Eastern Europe. The Council reached no decision, and action on the draft report, which had been proposed by the Department of State and designated NSC 46, was deferred.
The text of the draft report was not declassified in time to be included in this volume of documents.
